toxicity.summary_warning: All parts of the plant are highly poisonous. Even small amounts can cause severe poisoning, dangerous heart rhythm, paralysis, and death.

Neurological.name: Tingling and Numbness
Neurological.description: Early symptoms often include burning, tingling, or loss of sensation around the mouth, face, and limbs.

Neurological.name: Muscle Weakness
Neurological.description: Progressive weakness that may lead to difficulty moving or paralysis.

Neurological.name: Confusion and Dizziness
Neurological.description: Disorientation, lightheadedness, and impaired coordination may occur.

Cardiovascular.name: Dangerous Arrhythmias
Cardiovascular.description: Aconitine can disrupt electrical signaling in the heart, causing irregular and potentially fatal rhythms.

Cardiovascular.name: Low Blood Pressure
Cardiovascular.description: Severe hypotension may occur due to cardiovascular effects.

Cardiovascular.name: Rapid or Slow Heart Rate
Cardiovascular.description: Heart rate abnormalities can include both tachycardia and bradycardia.

Gastrointestinal.name: Abdominal Pain
Gastrointestinal.description: Stomach cramps and digestive discomfort may occur.

Gastrointestinal.name: Diarrhea
Gastrointestinal.description: Digestive irritation caused by toxic alkaloids.

toxic_parts.description: Among the most toxic parts, containing high concentrations of aconitine and related alkaloids.
Contain significant levels of toxic alkaloids and can cause poisoning through ingestion or contamination.

Contain concentrated alkaloids that remain dangerous.

Avoid touching eyes, mouth, or face after contact.

toxicity.summary_warning: All parts of water hemlock are highly poisonous, especially the roots. Ingestion can cause neurological symptoms, severe seizures, respiratory failure, and death.

Neurological.name: Seizures
Neurological.description: Violent and potentially continuous seizures are the primary life-threatening effect of cicutoxin poisoning.

Neurological.name: Tremors and Muscle Spasms
Neurological.description: Abnormal muscle activity and uncontrolled movements may occur.

Neurological.name: Confusion and Loss of Consciousness
Neurological.description: Severe poisoning may lead to altered mental state, coma, or unconsciousness.

Cardiovascular.name: Abnormal Heart Rhythm
Cardiovascular.description: Severe poisoning can disrupt normal cardiovascular function.

Cardiovascular.name: Blood Pressure Changes
Cardiovascular.description: Toxic effects may interfere with normal circulation.

Respiratory.name: Respiratory Failure
Respiratory.description: Seizures and nervous system disruption may impair breathing.

Gastrointestinal.description: Digestive symptoms may occur after ingestion.

Gastrointestinal.name: Abdominal Pain
Gastrointestinal.description: Stomach discomfort and cramping may appear early.

toxicity.summary_warning: Giant Hogweed sap contains powerful photosensitizing compounds. Contact followed by sunlight exposure can cause severe burns, blistering, and long-lasting skin sensitivity.

Skin.name: Large Blisters

Skin.name: Dark Skin Discoloration
